basis.timeout_smoke_result: generation_timeout invalid candidate response with timeout_count=1
basis.scope: WSL CPU local Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct, 9 M4 fixture samples, max_new_tokens=1200

policy: Raw and repaired full candidate runs should use the same timeout so quality comparisons are not biased by different generation budgets.
Use 240 seconds for the current 1.5B full 9-sample raw/repaired run; it leaves margin above the observed 168.264 second slowest sample while still bounding abnormal stalls.
Use 180 seconds for focused single-sample debugging after the model is warm; if the sample times out, rerun once at 240 seconds before treating it as a model/case failure.
Use 300 seconds only for first-run cold cache, slower CPU, or larger local candidates; record the reason in the experiment note before comparing against the 240 second baseline.
Use 1 second only to verify timeout plumbing; such output is a mechanism smoke and must not be included in model quality comparisons.

interpretation: The 240 second timeout reproduced the previous raw blocked result and the repaired fixture-pass result without generation timeouts or max token hits.
The repaired slowest sample reached 233.771 seconds, leaving only about 6.229 seconds of headroom under the 240 second timeout on this WSL CPU run.

Keep 240 seconds as the current default for same-environment 1.5B raw/repaired comparisons, but monitor max_generation_seconds on every rerun and use the documented 300 second probe tier when hardware, cache state, or model size changes.
This confirmation still does not promote the raw model; repaired output remains an explicit postprocess experiment rather than raw capability evidence.

timeout300_probe.purpose: Probe samples that were slow or boundary-sensitive under the 240 second full-run confirmation before expanding hardware, model size, or sample coverage.
interpretation: The 300 second probe completed both raw and repaired tracks without generation timeouts or max token hits.
The raw slowest probe sample reached 254.678 seconds, so 240 seconds remains a same-environment 9-fixture comparison default rather than a permanent cross-sample or cross-hardware constant.

Use the 300 second tier before judging slow probe samples, expanded sample sets, cold-cache runs, slower CPUs, or larger local candidates; record the reason and keep raw/repaired tracks on the same timeout tier when comparing quality.
The repaired pass remains a postprocess observation and does not promote raw model capability.

holdout300_probe.purpose: Observe a lightweight training-excluded holdout slice before expanding the local candidate sample set beyond the original 9 fixture samples.

task_metric_summary.main_observation: repaired improved citation alignment but still failed advisory action boundary and parameter update detail checks.
raw and repaired both produced schema-invalid JSON with a trailing-comma parse error on the mixer outlet holdout sample.
repaired restored citation and read-only-check structure for the navigation holdout sample.

interpretation: The lightweight holdout probe is the next sample-observation step after the 240 second full-run confirmation and the 300 second timeout probe; it uses one training-excluded sample per main task.
Both raw and repaired tracks completed without generation timeouts or max token hits, so the observed failures are output quality and JSON validity failures rather than timeout failures.
Raw remains blocked on schema validity, task validity, citation alignment, issue/action structure, and advisory boundary metrics.

Repaired is also blocked on this holdout slice because the mixer outlet ghost completion output remained schema-invalid and the compressor parameter update sample still failed task-level structure checks.

This result weakens the previous fixture-only repaired pass: --repair-hard-fields is useful for hard-field restoration but is not sufficient evidence for promotion or training use on training-excluded holdout samples.

code_changes_observed: local_transformers JSON extraction now retries after removing trailing commas before object/array closers and records json_cleanup_applied in generation metrics.
candidate_edit scaffold now derives ordered parameter_updates and multi-issue structure from evaluation.ordered_parameter_update_* and must_have issue paths.

task_metric_summary.main_observation: ordered parameter update scaffold and repair now make the compressor holdout sample pass after repair; raw remains task-invalid.
trailing-comma cleanup turns the mixer outlet output into schema-valid task-valid JSON while recording json_cleanup_applied.
repaired continues to restore citation and read-only-check structure for the navigation holdout sample.

interpretation: The focused fix removes the holdout probe schema-invalid blocker and lets the repaired track pass this 3-sample holdout slice.
Raw still remains blocked because compressor and docs navigation outputs fail task-level structure/citation requirements without repair.
The repaired pass is still a postprocess observation, not raw model promotion evidence; expanding to the full planned holdout should continue to preserve raw/repaired separation.

details: parameter_updates.pressure_drop_kpa keys must remain ordered as ['action', 'minimum_value']
parameter_updates.opening_percent keys must remain ordered as ['action', 'suggested_maximum']

task_metric_summary.main_observation: repaired improves the full holdout slice but still fails advisory_action_boundary_rate=0.6666666666666666 because one valve parameter update sample violates inner detail-key ordering.
all three full holdout ghost completion samples pass raw and repaired task checks without timeout.
repaired restores docs QA citation and read-only-check structure across all three planned holdout samples.

interpretation: The full planned holdout confirms the 3-sample fix1 result does not fully generalize: repaired improves task_valid_rate to 8/9 but remains blocked.
Raw improves versus the 3-sample probe but is still blocked at 5/9 task-valid, with suggest_flowsheet_edits as the main failure family.
The next holdout repair step should target ordered parameter_update detail keys such as minimum_value and suggested_maximum without treating repaired output as raw model capability.

code_changes_observed: candidate_edit scaffold now derives parameter_updates outer keys from ordered_parameter_update_detail_keys when ordered_parameter_update_keys is absent.
detail-key-only parameter update constraints now preserve action-first ordering for keys such as minimum_value and suggested_maximum.

details: parameter_updates.pressure_drop_kpa.minimum_value was repaired to true instead of a concrete threshold such as 10.
parameter_updates.opening_percent.suggested_maximum was repaired to true instead of a concrete upper bound such as 85.
citation locator was too broad because it pointed at artifact:flowsheet_document instead of diagnostics, valve unit, and latest_snapshot evidence.

task_metric_summary.main_observation: machine metrics passed after the detail-key ordering scaffold fix, but manual review blocks this result because the valve sample still used boolean parameter thresholds and overly broad citations.
ghost completion remains fully task-valid on the full holdout slice.
docs QA remains fully task-valid after repair on the full holdout slice.

interpretation: The targeted detail-key ordering fix let the old machine metric reach 9/9 task-valid, but manual review blocks the result and raw remains blocked at 5/9.
The result validates the need for stricter value and citation assertions before any repaired holdout result is treated as acceptable; it is not raw model promotion evidence.
Further promotion or training decisions still require raw improvement, manual review of repaired paths, and broader non-overlapping holdout coverage.

code_changes_observed: candidate citation scaffold now derives indexed citation ids and locators from must_have_json_paths.
repair-hard-fields replaces citation arrays when indexed citation assertions require a stable order and precise locator metadata.

accepted_observations: citations are repaired to diagnostics[0], diagnostics[1], flowsheet_document.units[0], and latest_snapshot locators.

task_metric_summary.main_observation: indexed citation scaffold repair brings all three full holdout suggest edits samples back to task-valid under the stricter value and citation assertions.
ghost completion remains fully task-valid on the full holdout slice.
docs QA remains fully task-valid after repair on the full holdout slice.

interpretation: The stricter full holdout repaired track reaches 9/9 task-valid after indexed citation scaffold repair, but raw remains blocked at 5/9.
This is still postprocess evidence, not raw model promotion evidence.
Further training or promotion decisions require raw improvement, broader non-overlapping holdout coverage, and continued manual review of repaired paths.

remaining_failure.summary: The repaired response still fails the second action target, citation order, parameter_updates payload, and patch ordering requirements.

task_metric_summary.main_observation: the non-overlapping cross-object reconnect plus pump parameter sample remains blocked after repair; citation_alignment_rate and advisory_action_boundary_rate stay at 0.5 for this two-sample slice.
both ghost completion samples pass raw and repaired, including the previously slow valve ambiguous no-tab sample.
docs QA repairs the source-conflict read_only_check and evidence-gap risk/status boundaries, but this remains postprocess evidence.

interpretation: The v2 non-overlapping probe confirms that raw remains blocked outside the original full holdout slice.
Repair improves task-valid from 2/6 to 5/6 but no longer reaches a fixture pass, because complex cross-object suggest edits still fails target, citation, and parameter patch requirements.

The next useful work is to inspect the failed cross-object suggest edits family and decide whether the scaffold is intentionally insufficient or whether model output strategy needs a stronger structured action plan.

holdout_probe_v2_scaffold_root_cause_rev.root_cause: The candidate scaffold and repair path reused action_index=0 for every candidate_edit, so the second action was repaired into the first stream reconnect action instead of the pump parameter update action.
secondary_scaffold_gaps: ordered connection_placeholder keys were not merged when the patch already contained connection_placeholder.expected_downstream_kind
minimum_reference_stream_id fell back to a boolean placeholder instead of the inferred upstream feed stream id
code_changes: scripts/run-radishmind-core-candidate.py now reads target, citation_ids, patch, connection_placeholder and parameter_updates with the current action_index
scripts/eval/core_candidate_scaffold.py now infers minimum_reference_stream_id from the same upstream stream helper used for reference_stream_id
scripts/check-radishmind-core-candidate-parameter-updates.py now locks the cross-object v2 sample's second action target, patch order, parameter_updates and citation_ids
interpretation: The previous v2 repaired blocker was a scaffold/repair coverage bug, not evidence that this specific sample requires a stronger model action plan.
Raw output remains blocked and still must not be promoted or used as training evidence.
A future real local_transformers rerun is optional for metric refresh, but the root-cause fix is already covered by committed dry-run and scaffold checks.

failure_classification.observed_raw_failure: The output stayed schema-valid but changed status to ok, dropped answers and issues, emitted only one candidate_edit, omitted the action target, and missed the second pump parameter action.
failure_classification.scaffold_context: The raw run used the pre-fix scaffold where the second candidate_edit duplicated action_index=0. This likely amplified the missing second-action failure, so this raw result should not be used alone to judge model action planning.
still_model_relevant: raw ignored the scaffold requirement to keep status=partial
raw dropped required answers and issues arrays
raw omitted a required target field even though candidate_edit requires target
failure_classification.recommended_followup: Refresh this sample only after the action-indexed scaffold fix if a new raw metric is needed; otherwise keep it as evidence that complex suggest edits requires stronger hard-field preservation.

failure_classification.observed_raw_failure: The output preserved the core unit target and parameter_updates payload, but changed status from partial to ok and left answers empty.
failure_classification.scaffold_context: The core action planning and ordered suggested_range were mostly correct, so this is not a parameter patch planning failure.
still_model_relevant: raw does not preserve required answers even for a simple one-action sample
failure_classification.recommended_followup: Treat as a hard-field/status preservation prompt or constrained-decoding candidate, not as a reason to add more training data yet.

failure_classification.observed_raw_failure: The answer follows the official docs over the forum suggestion, but omits the required read_only_check action.
failure_classification.scaffold_context: The generic docs QA prompt says proposed_actions are usually absent, while this sample explicitly requires read_only_check. That prompt tension likely contributed to the raw omission.
still_model_relevant: raw does not reliably preserve sample-specific required_action_kinds when the generic task wording points the other way
failure_classification.recommended_followup: Adjust docs QA prompt wording so sample-level required_action_kinds overrides the generic no-action default before treating this as a model capacity failure.

failure_classification.observed_raw_failure: The output recognizes insufficient evidence and keeps issues/proposed_actions/citation shape, but changes status from partial to ok and risk_level from medium to low.
failure_classification.scaffold_context: The prompt and scaffold already state partial and medium, so this is a direct raw hard-field preservation gap.
still_model_relevant: raw treats an authorization uncertainty as low risk even when the sample expects medium
failure_classification.recommended_followup: Keep this as a primary raw-failure probe for risk/status preservation and evaluate schema-constrained decoding or stronger hard-field freezing.

task_level_conclusions: suggest_ghost_completion is the only v2 task group with raw pass on both non-overlapping samples.
suggest_flowsheet_edits raw failures split into one stale-scaffold-affected complex sample and one simple top-level shape preservation failure.
answer_docs_question raw failures are not retrieval-source failures; they are required-action preservation and evidence-gap risk/status preservation failures.
holdout_probe_v2_raw_failure_review_2026.recommended_next_step: Do not train from this review. First remove prompt ambiguity around sample-level required actions and consider a constrained-decoding or hard-field-freeze experiment; rerun v2 raw only after those prompt/scaffold changes if updated metrics are needed.

holdout_probe_v2_prompt_policy_fix_2026_.target_failure: radish-answer-docs-question-docs-faq-forum-conflict-001 omitted the sample-required read_only_check action while still following official source precedence.
holdout_probe_v2_prompt_policy_fix_2026_.root_cause: The generic Radish docs QA guidance said proposed_actions are usually absent, while the sample-level expected_response_shape.required_action_kinds explicitly required read_only_check.
code_changes: scripts/run-radishmind-core-candidate.py now treats required_action_kinds as a mandatory sample-level rule instead of a conditional preference.
For Radish docs QA samples with required_action_kinds, the generic no-action wording is replaced by wording that requires the corresponding proposed_actions.
scripts/check-radishmind-core-candidate-prompt-policy.py locks this policy using the v2 docs source-conflict sample and an evidence-gap no-action sample.
interpretation: This fixes a prompt-policy ambiguity; it does not prove raw model improvement until local_transformers is rerun.
The evidence-gap no-action prompt still keeps the explicit no-action rule.
The docs source-conflict prompt now names read_only_check and no longer contains the generic no-action phrase.

holdout_probe_v2_hard_field_freeze_promp.goal: Make hard-field preservation observable in raw candidate prompts before rerunning local_transformers.
code_changes: scripts/eval/core_candidate_hard_field_freeze.py derives prompt-time frozen JSON path/value pairs from the candidate scaffold.
scripts/run-radishmind-core-candidate.py writes hard_field_freeze into prompt documents and renders the frozen fields in the output contract text.
scripts/check-radishmind-core-candidate-hard-field-freeze.py locks v2 evidence-gap, docs source-conflict and efficiency-range prompt freeze behavior.
scripts/audit-radishmind-core-candidate-freeze.py compares completed candidate responses against the prompt hard_field_freeze without rerunning a model.
frozen_field_scope: Top-level schema_version, project, task, status, risk_level and requires_confirmation.
Explicit no-action samples freeze proposed_actions as an empty array.
Required action kind and read_only_check confirmation boundaries from required_action_kinds.
Action target, patch, preview and apply only when the sample has explicit JSON path assertions for those fields.
Answer, issue and citation detail fields are not frozen when they only come from generic scaffold placeholders.
interpretation: This is a prompt-time contract, not --repair-hard-fields post-decode repair.
It should make future raw/repaired reruns easier to interpret because raw outputs can be audited against explicit frozen path/value constraints.
No local model metric is updated by this record; v2 raw/repaired metrics still require a same-manifest local_transformers rerun.
The prompt-time hard_field_freeze contract is present and auditable, but this 1.5B raw output still violates the frozen status and risk_level paths.
The output preserved schema shape, issues, no-action boundary and citations, so the remaining blocker is specifically hard-field obedience rather than retrieval or JSON validity.
Do not treat prompt-time freeze alone as sufficient for raw promotion; continue evaluating constrained decoding or keep raw/repaired separation with explicit freeze audits.
The repaired track fixes exactly the same status and risk_level paths that the raw freeze audit reported as violations.
Answers, issues, citations and the no-action boundary were already structurally usable in raw output and did not need additional repair for this sample.
This supports keeping --repair-hard-fields as an explicit post-decode engineering track while treating raw hard-field obedience as still blocked.
The prompt policy fix appears effective for this sample: raw now emits the sample-required read_only_check action and the freeze audit passes.
The remaining raw blocker moved to citation coverage: the model kept only the official docs citation and omitted the FAQ and forum citations required by the evaluation paths.
This should no longer be classified as a required-action preservation failure; it is now a multi-citation/source-context preservation failure.

repaired_result_before_scaffold_fix.manual_review_caveat: The machine-valid repaired output padded citations[1] and citations[2] with duplicate docs_page citations instead of restoring the expected FAQ and forum source-context citations.

holdout_probe_v2_single_docs_conflict_re.root_cause: When must_have_json_paths required only $.citations[index] existence without explicit citation ids, build_citation_scaffold generated artifact-2/artifact-3 from the primary artifact instead of using the sample golden_response citation order.
code_changes: scripts/run-radishmind-core-candidate.py now uses golden_response.citations by index when citation count is inferred from $.citations[index] path requirements.
scripts/check-radishmind-core-candidate-citation-scaffold.py locks the docs source-conflict docs/faq/forum citation id and locator order and verifies repair restores those citations.
scripts/check-repo.py now includes the citation scaffold check.
interpretation: The repaired track can make the sample machine-valid, but the pre-fix repaired output was not strong enough as manual evidence because it duplicated the primary docs citation.
This is a scaffold/repair coverage bug, not a raw model improvement.
A same-sample repaired rerun is needed if an updated tmp repaired summary with restored FAQ/forum citations is required.

interpretation.recommended_next_step: Keep raw and repaired tracks separate, use --sample-timeout-seconds 240 for the current local_transformers full raw/repaired 1.5B candidate runs, expand beyond the current 9 fixture samples, and manually review repaired paths before any training or promotion decision.
